title: 4-(2-Pyridylazo) Resorcinol, A Sensitive Amperometric Reagent for Ultramicro Deterrnination of Pr3+ and Nd3+
author: S. C. LAVALE
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of chemistry, University of Saugar, Sagar-470 003, M.P. 

  Manuscript received 19 July 1987, revised 15 February 1982, accepted 1   May 1982  

 4-(2-Pyridylazo) resorcinol has been employed successfully as an amperometric reagent for the precise determination of Pr 8+  and Nd 8+ . The reagent's sensitivity is high. 0.1409 mg of Pr 8+  and 0.1442 mg of Nd 8+  could be detected successfully in 10 ml solution of each ion with an error of less than ±0.8%. The titrations have been performed at pH 6.1+ 0.02 and an ionic strength of 0.5  M  [KCI]. The molar ratio of metal : PAR complexation is found to be 1 : 2. Titrations are not hampered by the presence of fairly large amounts of K + , Na + , NH \(_4^+\) , Mg2 + , TI + , Ca 2+ , Mo 6+ , CI - , CIO 4 , CH 3 COO - , NO 8  and SO \(_2^4\)  -  ions. Experimental and calculated statistical data support the utility of the method.
